calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents And Produce Documents Without Objections And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF ALBERT LEE'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents And Produce Documents Without Objections And For Monetary Sanctions is
Off calendar at the request of the moving party (per e-mail dated 7/25/19). (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents And Produce Documents Without Objections And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Continued To Jul-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Albert Lee, Esq. 415-215-6868 for Plaintiff.
David Hunter, Esq. 925-683-9258 of Hartsuyker & Stratman for Defendant.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALBERT LEE's Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories Requests For Production Of Documents And Produce Documents Without Objections And For Monetary Sanctions.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Mar-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Albert Lee, Esq, 415-215-6868 appearing on his own behalf as pro per Plaintiff;
Sonia C. Merida, Esq., Hartsuyker, Stratman et al 510-457-3484, appearing for Defendant Ernest Lopez, et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ERNEST LOPEZ'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

Defendant Ernest Lopez's CCP 473(b) motion to set aside entry of default is granted. No default judgment has been entered in the case and an attorney fault declaration is filed with the motion. As plaintiff is in pro per, he can have no legal fees to be reimbursed and his papers claim no costs.

Order is signed
